source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Nitrogen Bottle Explosion at MP 42 Delta Platform

context.region: Gulf of America, Main Pass Block 42

actions_taken: BSEE notified by phone and email at 1611 hours on 27 April 2025
Incident report submitted to BSEE eWell system on 06 May 2025
BSEE on-site investigation conducted on 20 May 2025
Seven remaining nitrogen bottles shipped to shore prior to investigator arrival
Cantium required all nitrogen cylinders on Gulf platforms to be inspected
event.summary: On 27 April 2025 at 1520 hours, a 12-bottle nitrogen rack on the top deck of MP 42 Delta platform experienced an explosion. One nitrogen bottle with corrosion at its base ruptured, causing the 'daisy-chained' tubing and isolation valves to be ripped from the remaining bottles, launching them across the platform. Four bottles went overboard into the Gulf; seven were scattered on deck. One bottle remained severely damaged in the aluminum rack. No personnel were injured. Damage included the aluminum rack, welding machine door, and section of solid decking. The bottles were at least 1 year overdue for inspection.
recommendations: Implement routine inspection and maintenance program for nitrogen bottles used offshore
Perform proper hazard analysis for nitrogen bottle storage and use
Inspect all nitrogen cylinders on platforms across the Gulf to identify similar deficiencies
Ensure nitrogen bottles are inspected on schedule to prevent corrosion-related failures
key_phrases: API RP 14 C safety device testing

threats.name: Nitrogen bottle corrosion and structural degradation

consequences.name: Explosion and projectile launch of nitrogen bottles
Structural damage to platform equipment and decking
Potential personnel injury from flying debris

controls.name: Nitrogen bottle inspection and maintenance program
controls.barrier_role: Detect corrosion and degradation before rupture
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance to identify defects early

supporting_text: the bottles were at least 1 year overdue for inspection
Had the bottle been properly inspected, the corrosion may have been noticed before the bottle had a chance to rupture

controls.name: Hazard analysis for nitrogen bottle storage and use
controls.barrier_role: Identify risks and implement appropriate controls
controls.lod_basis: Systematic hazard identification and risk assessment

supporting_text: No or inadequate hazard analysis/written job procedures - Had a proper hazard analysis been performed, the corrosion on the base of the bottle may have been noticed prior to the explosion

controls.name: Isolation valve design and redundancy
controls.barrier_role: Prevent cascade failure of interconnected bottles
controls.lod_basis: Engineering design to limit propagation of failure

supporting_text: the bottles were 'daisy chained' together, meaning that the bottles were all connected with 1/4 inch stainless steel tubing, connecting the isolation valves located on top of each bottle
once one nitrogen bottle ruptured, the force of the rupture ripped the 'daisy chained' tubing and isolation valves from the remaining bottles, causing them to explode as well

controls.name: Physical barriers and storage location
controls.barrier_role: Shield personnel and equipment from flying debris
controls.lod_basis: Physical separation and shielding from hazard

supporting_text: The 12-bottle nitrogen rack was initially located approximately 15 feet from the base of the crane, behind a 10 foot tall storage container and next to several connex boxes and toolboxes
The large container/storage boxes near the bottle rack likely prevented the exploding bottles from causing additional damage to the platform and/or injury to personnel
These witnesses were shielded by several large container toolboxes and Connex storage buildings on the top deck

Cantium notified the Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) by phone call and email to the afterhours engineer at 1611 hours on the same day, and submitted an incident report in the BSEE eWell Incident Reporting System on 06 May 2025

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
